data.name: arr
data.description: CLI tool that executes a command on a file change.
data.readme_txt:
Auto Read-Runner

arr [OPTIONS]
OPTIONS:
--command    | command run when file is modifyed
--interval   | interval in seconds the file is checked
--message    | message showen when file is edited
--no-message | disable showing message
--no-command | disable running command
--no-startup | disable file check on script start

copyright 2025 9JH1

Build:
git clone https://github.com/9jh1/arr && cd arr
gcc arr.c -o arr
clear
./arr

data.name: CS12
data.description: Computer Science Yr 12 code, CS12_database - a from scratch implementation of a database program, reads and writes memory to files, CS12_program - a dynamic burrito ordering program with a full TUI interface.

data.name: dwm
data.description: Dotfiles for my personal DWM (suckless dynamic window manager) build and config, this is used on my primary desktop computer
data.readme_txt:
This is made for my FreeBSD system, majority of the scripts will not work on non-bsd systems
unless you change the shebang to the correct path to bash (bsd uses a different binary folder).

This repo contains the code for custom builds of DWM, ST, DMENU and a few other bits and bobs.
all source code is within the source folder and all scripts/modules for the bars are in the 
script/bar/modules and script/bar/modules/dwm directorys.

This is my PERSONAL repo so I will not have user catering at the top of my priority list.
All of the scripts are custom. If you have any questions you can contact me through my 
website: https://x3hy.github.io, also check out the img/ folder for some screenshots,
don't expect these to be up to date.

Open the img/ folder for screenshots.

data.name: i3-lite
data.description: A configuration of the X11 based tiling window manager I3, this config uses custom builds of i3bar i3status to achieve its looks, this config is used pirmarily on my laptop.

data.name: plib
data.description: A static, header only argument parsing library optimized for user QOL.
data.readme_txt:
*PLib 6.1.6*
**Documentation** : https://github.com/x3hy/plib/wiki
-------------------------
PLib is a C library made to make parsing command line arguments easier. The Latest version of plib uses zero memory allocation and is a completely header-only library which makes compilation a breeze.

Features:
- Header only library 
- Roughly *~400* lines of code.
- Completly **static allocation**.
- **Optimized** for user control.
- Very simple usage and instal.lation.
- Extensive **Error handling**
--------------------------

**Download/Install:** 
You *only* require the *plib6.h* file which you can download locally like so:

**Edge:** 
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/x3hy/plib/refs/heads/main/plib6.h

**Stable:**
wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/x3hy/plib/refs/tags/6.1.7/plib6.h

--------------------------
*example.c* contains quickstart code, please view the documentation link provided at the top of the project if you're stuck.

data.name: x3hy
data.description: Config files for my GitHub profile.
data.readme_txt:
I'm a low-level C developer. I write open source, free simple and optimized software. I love free (*as in freedom*) open source software and community driven projects. Some of my favorite software is made by *suckless software* and *GNU tools*. I value privacy over everything, I despise subscription services. The modern digital world is a mess, having to navigate surveillance, censorship, capitalism, and the ever increasing use of artificial intelligence. Put the power back in the hands of the people. I enjoy minimalism and pretty looking code, I strive to find simple and compact solutions to advanced problems.

Sources:
https://tools.suckless.org/dmenu/
https://dwm.suckless.org/
https://st.suckless.org/
https://suckless.org/
https://www.gnu.org/

Instead of reading any more drivel about my skills you can view my projects below.
